Package org.aavso.tools.vstar.util

Class AbstractExtremaFinder

java.lang.Object
org.aavso.tools.vstar.util.AbstractExtremaFinder
All Implemented Interfaces:
IInteruptible
Direct Known Subclasses:
ApacheCommonsBrentOptimiserExtremaFinder, ApacheCommonsDerivativeBasedExtremaFinder
public abstract class AbstractExtremaFinder extends Object implements IInteruptible

    Description
    protected int[]
    determineInitialSearchRangeForMaximum()
    Find the first numerically larger values either side of the numerically smallest (so, astronomical magnitude maximum) value.

    Find the first numerically smaller values either side of the numerically largest (so, astronomical magnitude minimum) value.

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractExtremaFinder

      public AbstractExtremaFinder(List<ValidObservation> obs, org.apache.commons.math.analysis.UnivariateRealFunction function, ICoordSource timeCoordSource, double zeroPoint)
      Constructor
      Parameters:
      obs - The list of observations modeled by the function.
      function - An Apache Commons Math Univariate function.
      timeCoordSource - Time coordinate source.
      zeroPoint - The zeroPoint to be added to the extreme time result.

  • Method Details

    • find

      public abstract void find(org.apache.commons.math.optimization.GoalType goal, int[] bracketRange) throws AlgorithmError
      Find the extremum according to the goal over a given time range.
      Parameters:
      goal - Minimum or maximum?
      bracketRange - The inclusive time range indices within which to look for the extremum.
      Throws:
      AlgorithmError

      Description copied from interface: IInteruptible
      Signals that a task has been interrupted.

    • determineInitialSearchRangeForMinimum

      protected int[] determineInitialSearchRangeForMinimum()
      Find the first numerically smaller values either side of the numerically largest (so, astronomical magnitude minimum) value.
      Returns:
      an inclusive range that brackets the astronomical minimum

    • determineInitialSearchRangeForMaximum

      protected int[] determineInitialSearchRangeForMaximum()
      Find the first numerically larger values either side of the numerically smallest (so, astronomical magnitude maximum) value.
      Returns:
      an inclusive range that brackets the astronomical maximum

      protected int getNumericallyMinimumMagnitudeIndex()

    • toMinimaString

      public String toMinimaString() throws AlgorithmError
      Return a string representation of the minimum magnitude (numerical maximum) generated by the function.
      Returns:
      The minimum string.
      Throws:
      AlgorithmError - if there is an error in minima determination.

    • toMaximaString

      public String toMaximaString() throws AlgorithmError
      Return a string representation of the maximum magnitude (numerical minimum) generated by the function.
      Returns:
      The maximum string.
      Throws:
      AlgorithmError - if there is an error in maxima determination.

    • toExtremumString

      protected String toExtremumString(String titleKey, org.apache.commons.math.optimization.GoalType goal, org.apache.commons.math.analysis.UnivariateRealFunction function, int[] bracketRange) throws AlgorithmError
      Return a string representation of the extreme magnitude (numerical opposite of magnitude) generated by the function.
      Parameters:
      goal - The goal (numericallyMinMagIndex, numericallyMaxMagIndex).
      function - The function for which the extremum is required.
      bracketRange - The inclusive range within which to look for the extremum.
      Returns:
      The extremum string.
      Throws:
      AlgorithmError - if there is an error in extrema determination.
